project-1.title: Ikea Kvartett
project-1.subtitle: An exploration of brand, information architecture, and tactile interaction
project-1.description: In an era of digital overload, I designed this analog quartet game to explore information architecture. Inspired by the IKEA store’s unique blend of function and togetherness, I distilled their massive product ecosystem into a playful, tactile system. By moving discovery off the screen and into the hands of the shopper, this game taps into classic nostalgia to turn a quick shopping trip into a more meaningful, shared journey—proving that sometimes, the most effective interface isn’t a screen, but a deck of cards.
project-1.challenge: I’ve always been amazed by the total experience of the IKEA store—the journey, the restaurant, and the way it blends function with social connection. However, as our lives become increasingly digital, I wanted to create an analog extension of that experience. My goal was to design a tactile, social bridge that keeps the spirit of the store alive, encouraging visitors to pause, connect, and explore the IKEA catalog in a way that feels intentional and calm.
project-1.process: A classic Quartet game. It taps into the nostalgia of the in-store experience and aligns with IKEA’s core value of "togetherness." By moving product discovery off the screen and into the hands of the shopper, I’ve created a moment of play that fits perfectly with the store’s social environment—turning a quick shopping trip into a more meaningful, shared journey.

project-2.title: Wie Gaat Eerst? (Who goes first?)
project-2.subtitle: Building nurse resilience by integrating a prioritization game that develops instinctive cognitive schemas to prevent mental exhaustion from information overload.
